hero.title: AlgoCoach: Your next step for every algorithm problem
hero.description: Move from a blank editor to a working solution with structured practice, real code execution, and feedback that explains what to try next.

announcement.title: No sign-in required. Start with a guided problem.

hero.tip: Practice in JavaScript or Python, directly in your browser.

image_invert.alt: AlgoCoach guided algorithm practice workspace in dark mode

introduce.title: A complete learning loop, not another answer box
introduce.label: Teach · Practice · Assess · Review
introduce.description: AlgoCoach keeps the explanation, coding environment, feedback, assessment, and review plan connected so each attempt produces a useful next action.

items.title: Teach the idea
items.description: Understand the pattern, constraints, and trade-offs before reaching for a finished answer.

items.title: Practice with real feedback
items.description: Write and run JavaScript or Python, inspect test results, and locate the exact failing case.

items.title: Assess independently
items.description: Use timed, hint-free exercises to check whether the skill transfers without coaching.

items.title: Review what matters
items.description: Turn mistakes and hint usage into focused review cards and a clearer next practice target.

benefits.title: Designed around the hard parts of algorithm learning
benefits.label: Why it helps
benefits.description: The coach intervenes where learners most often stall: starting a solution, interpreting failures, and retaining the pattern after a problem is complete.

items.title: Start with structure
items.description: Difficulty, constraints, function signatures, and starter code reduce setup work without removing the thinking.

items.title: Recover from mistakes faster
items.description: Diagnostics connect compiler errors or failed tests to a concrete cause, then suggest a targeted next check.

items.title: Build durable recall
items.description: Counterexamples, assessments, and review cards expose weak spots and bring them back into the learning plan.

features.title: Coaching tools that preserve the learning
features.description: Every feature is built to help you reason, test, and improve without silently replacing your work.

items.title: Smart problem setup
items.description: Parse a prompt, identify likely difficulty and constraints, then prepare a language-specific starter template.

items.title: Immersive code practice
items.description: Edit, run, submit, inspect console output, and compare sample or full test results in one workspace.

items.title: Three-level hint ladder
items.description: Reveal a concept, an approach, or pseudocode one step at a time without exposing a complete solution.

items.title: Evidence-based diagnosis
items.description: Tie feedback to actual syntax errors, runtime failures, or test output instead of guessing.

items.title: Counterexample generation
items.description: Challenge an assumption with a small input that makes the current logic fail.

items.title: Progress and review
items.description: Track practice completion, hint use, corrections, assessments, and topic mastery over time.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: What to expect from the visitor-first MVP.

items.question: Do I need an account?
items.answer: No. The demo learning loop works without sign-in and stores your learning state in this browser.

items.question: Which languages can I practice?
items.answer: The MVP supports JavaScript and Python, with code executed in an isolated browser worker.

items.question: Will hints give away the answer?
items.answer: Hints are progressive: first the concept, then the solution direction, then pseudocode. They are designed to stop short of a complete implementation.

items.question: What happens without an AI key?
items.answer: Code execution and tests still work, while AI feedback reports that the provider is not configured instead of returning simulated advice.

items.question: Can I import my own problem?
items.answer: Yes. You can review a parsed draft with estimated difficulty, constraints, function signature, and starter code. The system does not invent hidden tests for imported prompts.

items.question: Where is my learning data stored?
items.answer: Visitor data stays in the browser. After sign-in, learning profiles, code runs, feedback, assessments, and events are synchronized to the account database.

cta.title: Turn the next problem into a learning step
cta.description: Choose a goal, open today's task, and start with the smallest useful hint.
